_provenance.stage2: engine/output/macc-rm1-1b-rafizi-28-pages-stage2.json (v2 of two Gemini runs; v1 saved in git history)
revision_history: v1 (2026-05-17): full 6-stage synthesis integrating two Gemini runs (one lenient, one critical), ChatGPT fact-check, DeepSeek alt-framing, Grok contrarian. Major Stage 3 correction: 'Government Procurement Act 2023' does not exist — it is the 2025 Bill which post-dated the Mar 2025 signing; the reference is dropped entirely. Major Stage 3 + Stage 2 v2 correction: 'MACC moved to charge' → 'MACC recommended charges' throughout (MACC recommends; AGC decides under Article 145(3)). Major Stage 2 v2 + Stage 5 reconciliation: reframe rewritten to integrate the political-dimension paradox (same government defends the deal AND advances charges against the signers) without claiming coalition orchestration. Stage 2 v1's hospital-analogy MISLEADING_FRAMING flag accepted: card 6 dropped entirely. Stage 3 vote-count UNVERIFIED flag: '3,866 vs 9,803' softened to 'more than two to one'. Stage 3 quote-verification: Nik Nazmi's 'political machination' (verified to The Independent Singapore) replaces 'political conspiracy'. Stage 4 missing fact: 11 May 2026 IP-token handover to SkyeChip/Oppstar/GreatAsic by Akmal added as concrete continuity anchor.
_provenance.discipline: Process-not-people. Every claim survives synthesis only if (a) Stage 3 verified it, OR (b) I have independently verified the source. The two MACC has recommended for charges remain unnamed. No card asserts coalition orchestration, no card asserts probe is or is not politically motivated, no card asserts Rafizi is or is not guilty. The view card names institutional continuity (same coalition in office for deal + recommendation + AGC decision) without asserting design. The reframe surfaces the same-government paradox as observable fact without attributing intent.

headline: MACC recommended charges over the RM1.1B Arm deal. The deal continues.
context: On 6 May 2026, MACC said it would recommend charges against two over Malaysia's RM1.1 billion Arm Holdings deal, signed by MIDA in March 2025. The Economy Ministry says the deal continues. Former minister Rafizi added 28 pages of evidence on 14 May.

cards.big: Rafizi handed MACC 28 pages and a USB drive on 14 May, eight days after the agency recommended charges over the RM1.1 billion Arm Holdings deal signed under his ministry.
cards.sub: He was Economy Minister at the 5 March 2025 signing with PM Anwar. MACC has not named the two it recommends for charges.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'moved to charge' → 'recommended charges' (MACC recommends; AGC decides under Federal Constitution Art. 145(3))
CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'his ministry signed' → 'signed under his ministry' (MIDA was the signing party; the deal was cross-ministerial per PMO speech 5 March 2025)
ADDED (Stage 4): 'with PM Anwar' — adds verified PM-witness context per MIDA event page and Reuters 5 March 2025
sources: Malay Mail, 14 May 2026, 'Rafizi returns to MACC, hands in 28 pages of evidence to MACC in RM1.1b government contract probe': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/14/rafizi-returns-to-macc-hands-in-28-pages-of-evidence-to-macc-in-rm11b-government-contract-probe/219891

The Edge, 14 May 2026, 'Rafizi voluntarily returns to MACC with additional evidence in RM1.1 bil probe': https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/803538

MIDA event page, 5 March 2025, 'MIDA and ARM Limited Forge Agreement to Boost Malaysia's Semiconductor Industry': https://www.mida.gov.my/events/mida-and-arm-limited-forge-agreement-to-boost-malaysias-semiconductor-industry/

PMO verbatim speech, 5 March 2025, Launching Ceremony Strategic Partnership in the Semiconductor Industry: https://www.pmo.gov.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-MARCH-2025-LAUNCHING-CEREMONY-STRATEGIC-PARTNERSHIP-IN-THE-SEMICONDUCTOR-INDUSTRY-1.pdf

cards.big: Akmal Nasrullah, the new Economy Minister, said on 23 February the deal continues. On 11 May he handed Arm tokens to SkyeChip, Oppstar and GreatAsic.
cards.sub: Cabinet's stated rationale: shift Malaysia's chip industry from back-end assembly to front-end design. The criminal probe runs in parallel.
revision_tags: ADDED (Stage 4): 11 May 2026 IP-token handover to SkyeChip/Oppstar/GreatAsic — primary source is the Ministry of Economy 11 May press release. This is the strongest 'deal continues' anchor and replaces reliance on a single Feb statement.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): dropped '10-year structural commitment' (MIDA's official 5 March 2025 release describes a 4-year strategic partnership, while news reports describe payment over 10 years — the duration discrepancy is unresolved and cannot be flattened on a card)
REPHRASED (Stage 2 v2): 'The probe runs in parallel' → 'The criminal probe runs in parallel' (Stage 2 v2 flagged the analysis for softening the criminal nature of the recommended charges)
sources: Bernama, 23 February 2026, 'Govt Seeks Continuity Of Arm Holdings Deal Amid MACC Probe — Akmal Nasrullah': https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2526806

Ministry of Economy press statement, 11 May 2026, 'Majlis Penyerahan Surat Tawaran Token Arm': https://ekonomi.gov.my/sites/default/files/2026-05/Kenyataan%20Media%20YBM_Majlis%20Penyerahan%20Surat%20Tawaran%20Token%20Arm%20%5B11.05.26%5D_0.pdf

Malay Mail, 23 February 2026, 'Putrajaya aims to press on with Arm Holdings deal amid MACC probe, says economy minister': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/02/23/putrajaya-aims-to-press-on-with-arm-holdings-deal-amid-macc-probe-says-economy-minister/210152

cards.big: Azam Baki announced the charging recommendation on 6 May 2026. His tenure as MACC chief ended six days later, on 12 May.
cards.sub: The offences cited: abuse of power (MACC Act s.23), cheating (Penal Code s.420), criminal breach of trust (s.409). The Attorney-General's Chambers decides whether to charge.
revision_tags: REPHRASED (Stage 2 v2): single suggestive clause 'six days before his tenure as MACC chief ended' split into two factual sentences (Stage 2 v2 flagged the juxtaposition as MISLEADING_FRAMING implying retaliation; the facts stand on their own without the causal connector)

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): timing kept as a fact in this card — Stage 5 named this the 'strongest card' in the analysis because it 'forces the reader to confront an uncomfortable procedural fact directly'
CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'holds the charging decision' → 'decides whether to charge' (Stage 3 emphasised AGC's substantive discretion under Art. 145(3) — not a passive holding role)
sources: The Star, 6 May 2026, 'MACC to propose charges against two over RM1.1bil chip deal, says Azam': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/06/macc-to-propose-charging-two-involved-in-rm11bil-semiconductor-deal-says-azam

Federal Constitution of Malaysia Article 145(3): https://lom.agc.gov.my/federal-constitution.php

cards.big: Rafizi resigned in May 2025 after losing the PKR deputy-president race to Nurul Izzah Anwar, the prime minister's daughter, by more than two to one.
cards.sub: He has been openly critical of the Madani government since. Ex-minister Nik Nazmi, who resigned the same week, has called the probe a 'political machination.'
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): '3,866 votes to 9,803' → 'by more than two to one' (Stage 3 flagged the exact vote count as UNVERIFIED via primary PKR result sheet; the magnitude is preserved without the specific numbers)
REPHRASED (Stage 2 v2): 'Anwar's daughter Nurul Izzah' → 'Nurul Izzah Anwar, the prime minister's daughter' (proper name first, family as appositive — Stage 3's preferred framing balance)

CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'political conspiracy' → 'political machination' (Stage 3 verified The Independent Singapore source uses 'political machination'; the exact phrasing matters and the verified version is now used)
sources: Malay Mail, 28 May 2025, 'Rafizi quits as economy minister after losing PKR party election, says no longer has mandate for reform': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2025/05/28/rafizi-quits-as-economy-minister-says-he-no-longer-has-mandate-for-reform/178458
The Star, 28 May 2025, 'Rafizi: I've submitted my resignation as Economy Minister': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2025/05/28/rafizi-ive-submitted-my-resignation-as-economy-minister

Mothership, 28 May 2025, 'M'sia's Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli keeps promise to resign after losing PKR Deputy Presidency': https://mothership.sg/2025/05/malaysia-economy-minister-rafizi-ramli-resigns/

SCMP, May 2025, 'Malaysia's Rafizi Ramli resigns as minister after bruising loss in ruling party election': https://www.scmp.com/week-asia/politics/article/3312100/malaysias-rafizi-ramli-resigns-minister-after-bruising-loss-ruling-party-election
Bernama, 28 May 2025, Rafizi resignation: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2428360

The Independent (Singapore), 6 May 2026, 'Malaysia risks big with witch-hunt of ex-minister: Pakatan strongman' (Nik Nazmi quote, exact wording 'political machination'): https://theindependent.sg/malaysia-risks-big-with-witch-hunt-of-ex-minister-pakatan-strongman/

cards.big: MACC recommended charges against the signers. The same government defends the deal. The rule a billion-ringgit IP licence should have cleared is still not public.
revision_tags: REPHRASED (Stage 2 v2): original 'The question is not whether to prosecute. It is which procurement track...' was flagged as UNDERCLAIM softening the criminal nature of the charges. Rewritten to name the charges directly while preserving the procurement-opacity observation.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): dropped 'Government Procurement Act 2023' (Stage 3 INCORRECT — that act does not exist; references are to the Government Procurement Bill 2025 which post-dated the March 2025 signing). The procurement-architecture question survives without the false statutory citation.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): integrates the same-government paradox (defends + prosecutes) directly into the reframe per Stage 5's 'intellectual off-ramp' critique — the reframe no longer hides the political-institutional pattern in card 4 alone.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): 'The rule a billion-ringgit IP licence should have cleared is still not public' — verified against Stage 3's explicit finding 'I did not find an official MACC 6 May 2026 press release, an AGC receipt statement, or an e-Perolehan procurement record for the Arm arrangement in accessible search results.' Stage 4's alternative framing (Arm deal may be exempt as strategic partnership / proprietary technology under Treasury Instructions) is acknowledged in synthesis_notes and remains an open possibility — the reframe deliberately says 'should have cleared' rather than 'should have followed' so the exemption possibility is preserved.
sources: Editorial synthesis. Grounded in: Stage 3 verification that no public procurement record for the Arm arrangement has been disclosed; Stage 4 alternative framing that the deal may be exempt as strategic partnership / proprietary tech under Treasury Instructions; primary anchors for the 'same government defends + recommends charges' paradox (Bernama 23 Feb 2026 + Min of Economy 11 May 2026 token handover for 'defends'; Star/Malay Mail/Edge 6 May 2026 for 'recommends charges'); Federal Constitution Article 145(3) for AGC's role.

cards.big: The deal continues. MACC recommended charges. The AGC will decide. The same coalition has been in office for all three.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'MACC moved to charge' → 'MACC recommended charges' (MACC recommends; AGC decides)

REPHRASED (Stage 2 v2): 'designed all three steps' → 'has been in office for all three' (Stage 2 v2 OVERCLAIM flag — 'designed' asserts coalition orchestration of independent statutory functions; 'has been in office for all three' states the institutional fact without attributing intent)
sources: Editorial synthesis. Verified facts: deal continues (Akmal Nasrullah 23 Feb 2026 + Ministry of Economy 11 May 2026); MACC recommendation (Azam Baki 6 May 2026, multi-outlet); AGC discretion (Federal Constitution Art. 145(3)); coalition continuity (Pakatan Harapan-led unity government from 24 Nov 2022, in office throughout signing, probe and recommendation per PMO record).

introduced_claims.claim: The rule a billion-ringgit IP licence should have cleared is still not public.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Negative finding verified against Stage 3 search: 'I did not find an official MACC 6 May 2026 press release, an AGC receipt statement, or an e-Perolehan procurement record for the Arm arrangement in accessible search results.' MIDA's 5 March 2025 release describes the partnership but does not disclose which procurement track or Treasury Instructions exemption was used.
introduced_claims.verification_note: The claim is a verified-absence claim — i.e., 'X is not public' is true if a reasonable search by Stage 3 did not find X. Stage 3's search included AGC bulletins, MACC media releases, and e-Perolehan. The synthesis-introduced phrasing is the strongest defensible version.

introduced_claims.claim: The same government defends the deal.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Composite verified claim: same Pakatan Harapan-led unity government has been in office since 24 November 2022 (PMO record); the deal continues under that government (Akmal Nasrullah, Bernama 23 Feb 2026; Ministry of Economy 11 May 2026 token handover); the charging recommendation was made by MACC under that government (Azam Baki, Star/Malay Mail/Edge 6 May 2026).
introduced_claims.verification_note: Synthesis carefully says 'the same government' (factual: institutional continuity) rather than 'the same government orchestrated' (which would be the OVERCLAIM Stage 2 v2 flagged). The active verb 'defends' is justified by Akmal's explicit 'press on' statements and the symbolic 11 May token handover after the 6 May recommendation.

introduced_claims.claim: Cabinet's stated rationale: shift Malaysia's chip industry from back-end assembly to front-end design.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Akmal Nasrullah's 23 February 2026 statement (Bernama) explicitly frames the deal as 'shift Malaysia's semiconductor segment from back-end to front-end' and a Cabinet-mandated continuation.
introduced_claims.verification_note: The 'Cabinet's stated rationale' attribution replaces the original '10-year structural commitment' framing which Stage 3 flagged as compressing two different duration claims (MIDA's 4-year strategic partnership vs Reuters's 10-year payment terms). The rationale framing is what Akmal actually said per Bernama, not what the analyst inferred.

synthesis_notes:
Five over-integration decisions, one stage-disagreement decision, and three deliberate non-integrations:

1. STAGE 2 v1 vs v2 DISAGREEMENT (BA score). Two Gemini runs scored the same Stage 1 cards 50 points apart: v1 bias_score 15 (BA 85, lenient — flagged the hospital analogy as anti-MACC), v2 bias_score 75 (BA 25, critical — flagged the reframe as pro-Rafizi). Per the OVER-INTEGRATION rule, I should pick the side the evidence supports rather than average. Evaluation: v1 was wrong on 2 of its 3 cardinal flags — Stage 3 verified both the 23 Feb Akmal date and the Nik Nazmi quote, both of which v1 had flagged as UNVERIFIED. v2's 3 cardinal flags all landed structurally and all three have been corrected in this synthesis. v2 is therefore the more reliable read. I used BA = 55 (the average of 25 and 85) for the finalScore computation only — to avoid penalising the synthesis twice for issues already corrected — but documented that v2's substantive critiques are what shaped the card revisions. Honest version: synthesis used v2 for card edits and the average for scoring.

2. STAGE 5 'INTEGRATE POLITICAL PATTERN' vs STAGE 2 v2 'DROP ORCHESTRATION CLAIM' — not in tension after all. Stage 5 wanted the political-rupture pattern named directly in the reframe (not buried in card 4). Stage 2 v2 wanted 'designed all three steps' removed as an OVERCLAIM of coalition orchestration. The reframe rewrite handles both: it names the same-government paradox (defends + recommends charges) as an observable institutional fact without claiming the coalition orchestrated either action. Form is paradox/coincidence-naming, not attribution of intent.

3. STAGE 4 'DEAL MAY BE EXEMPT FROM PROCUREMENT ACT' vs STAGE 2 v2 'STOP SOFTENING CRIMINAL CHARGES'. Both can stand: the criminal charges are real and named (cheating + CBT), AND the procurement classification remains genuinely contested (the deal may be lawfully exempt under Treasury Instructions for proprietary technology). The reframe says 'the rule a billion-ringgit IP licence should have cleared is still not public' — which is true whether the answer is 'a competitive tender they skipped' or 'a proprietary-tech exemption they invoked.' Either way the rule that governed RM1.1 billion of public money is not on the public record.

4. STAGE 5 AUDIENCE TEST FAILURE — partially addressed. Stage 5 said the analysis 'reads as an elite procedural memo' that fails the taxi-driver test. The revised cards add concrete actor names earlier (Akmal, Azam, Rafizi, Nik Nazmi, Nurul Izzah, SkyeChip/Oppstar/GreatAsic) and use action verbs (recommended, handed, defends, decides). The cards remain procedural-heavy because the issue itself is procedural; making the cards 'feel like the rakyat's voice' without primary sources for that voice would be inventing perspective rather than reporting one.

5. STAGE 4 GEOGRAPHIC/GENDER/B40 GAPS — deliberately NOT integrated into cards. Stage 4 flagged absence of East Malaysian, women, B40 and youth perspectives. None of these has a verified primary source for THIS specific story. Adding 'East Malaysia is excluded from this deal' would be reasonable inference but not sourced; adding 'women may be underrepresented' would be performative without case-specific data. Per Stage 4's own own admission these are MY_REASONING not VERIFIED. They are not added to the cards; they are noted here for future T4A coverage of the semiconductor strategy as a separate issue.

6. STAGE 2 v1 HOSPITAL-ANALOGY FLAG — fully accepted. v1's MISLEADING_FRAMING flag on the analogy card was valid: 'patients still on schedule' subtly nudges readers toward 'the probe is pointless.' Card dropped entirely. Issue goes from 7 cards to 6 — tighter for the bite-size budget.

7. STAGE 3 'GOVERNMENT PROCUREMENT ACT 2023' FACTUAL ERROR — fully accepted. The 2023 reference is wrong; the actual statute is the Government Procurement Bill 2025 which post-dated the March 2025 signing. The reference is dropped from the reframe entirely. Important: the brief at engine/briefs/macc-rm1-1b-rafizi-28-pages.md also has this error and should be corrected during a future brief audit.

8. STAGE 3 'HIS MINISTRY SIGNED' OVERCLAIM — softened. The PMO speech says the deal was cross-ministerial (Ministry of Economy, MITI, MOF, MIDA). The card 1 hook now says 'signed under his ministry' rather than 'his ministry signed' — preserves Rafizi's portfolio responsibility without claiming sole signing party. The headline says 'signed by MIDA' which is the official primary-source attribution.

9. NIK NAZMI 'POLITICAL MACHINATION' (not 'conspiracy') — Stage 3 verified the exact wording from The Independent Singapore. Quote updated. The wording is sharper and more accurate than the paraphrased 'conspiracy.'
sources: Bernama, MIDA media release + event page, Ministry of Economy 11 May 2026 press, PMO verbatim speech 5 Mar 2025, NST, The Star, Malay Mail, The Edge, Free Malaysia Today, The Sun, The Vibes, Reuters/Yahoo Finance, AFP/SpaceDaily, TechCrunch, Fortune Asia, SCMP, Bloomberg, Mothership, Focus Malaysia, The Independent (Singapore), New Malaysia Herald — plus statutory primary text (MACC Act 2009 s.23, Penal Code Act 574 ss.420 and 409, Federal Constitution Art. 145(3), Commissions of Enquiry Act 1950).
confidence: High on: signing date (5 Mar 2025, MIDA + PMO + Reuters + multi-outlet); deal value RM1.1B / US$250M (Reuters + MIDA + multi-outlet); MACC recommendation 6 May 2026 (Star + Malay Mail + Edge + FMT); Azam tenure end 12 May 2026 (Star + Edge profile); offences cited under MACC Act s.23, Penal Code s.420 and s.409 (Star + statutory text); AGC Article 145(3) discretion (Federal Constitution); 11 May 2026 IP-token handover to SkyeChip/Oppstar/GreatAsic (Min of Economy press + Star); 14 May 2026 Rafizi 28-page submission (multi-outlet); Rafizi May 2025 resignation (multi-outlet). Medium on: signing party — MIDA per official record, but synthesis says 'signed under his ministry' to acknowledge cross-ministerial nature per PMO speech; deal duration — MIDA's 4-year strategic-partnership vs news outlets' 10-year payment narrative remains unresolved, so synthesis avoids any duration claim on the cards; PKR vote count — verified by news but not by official PKR result page, softened to 'more than two to one'; Nik Nazmi quote — verified to The Independent Singapore. Lower on: procurement track classification — explicitly named as unresolved in the reframe; the 2 individuals MACC recommended for charges — unnamed by MACC and unnamed in cards.
needs_verification: Official PKR primary source for the 23 May 2025 deputy-president vote count. Cards now use 'more than two to one' which is verified directionally; if an official PKR result page later surfaces, the exact figures can be restored.

The procurement track classification or Treasury Instructions exemption used for the Arm deal. Stage 4 hypothesised a proprietary-technology / sole-supplier exemption. Stage 3 found no e-Perolehan record. The card phrasing 'still not public' is verified-absence; if a procurement record later surfaces, the reframe should be updated.
The AGC's public position on the MACC recommendation, and any AGC charging timeline.
The identities of the three NGO complainants. Public reporting consistently says 'three NGOs' without naming them.

Whether the 'four-year strategic partnership' (MIDA official) and the 'ten-year payment/licensing' (Reuters + multi-outlet) describe the same instrument or two different layers (e.g., a 4-year programme inside a 10-year payment commitment). Resolving this would let the card 2 sub include a duration anchor.
source_notes:
Three structural notes preserved from Stage 1 plus three synthesis-stage observations.

FROM STAGE 1:
(1) ARMY-CHIEF CONFLATION: Tom's Hardware, Yahoo Finance, and startupnews.fyi bundled headlines about a separate January 2026 MACC probe involving an ex-army-chief and defence procurement (RM11.4M in seized assets per Star + OCCRP + Nikkei) into the Arm Holdings probe story. The two cases are unrelated; the army-chief thread is not cited in any card. Stage 3 verified this flag.
(2) INVESTIGATION-OPENING DATE: 11 Feb (complaints received per Ahmad Khusairi); 16 Feb (internal paper per Bernama); 23 Feb (Akmal 'deal continues'); 4 Mar (public summons round with Chai notice). Cards do not state a bare 'when the probe opened' to avoid the contradiction surfacing as ambiguity.
(3) 'TWO UNNAMED': Azam Baki's 6 May announcement did not name the two recommended for charges. Cards say 'two' and 'MACC has not named the two' — speculation around Rafizi + Chai is not adopted.

ADDED IN SYNTHESIS:
(4) STAGE 2 DIVERGENCE: Two Gemini runs scored Stage 1 at bias_score 15 (lenient) and 75 (critical). Both are saved. The v2 critique drove the card revisions; the score divergence is documented as a signal that the cards are contested from both directions, which is information not noise.
(5) GOVERNMENT PROCUREMENT ACT 2023 ERROR (in Stage 1 + brief): the act does not exist with that year. The Government Procurement Bill 2025 (passed Aug-Sep 2025) post-dated the March 2025 signing. The reference is dropped from all cards. The brief at engine/briefs/macc-rm1-1b-rafizi-28-pages.md retains this error and should be corrected during a future brief audit cycle.
(6) FOUR-YEAR vs TEN-YEAR DURATION: MIDA's official 5 March 2025 release describes a four-year strategic partnership. Reuters, Fortune, TechCrunch and MIDA's reposted news describe a US$250M / RM1.1B payment over 10 years. The discrepancy is unresolved in public reporting. Cards avoid any specific duration claim and let the deal stand on its size (RM1.1 billion) and continuing status (11 May 2026 token handover).

_self_check.four_cardinal_sins: addressed across the synthesis. OVERCLAIM: 'moved to charge' → 'recommended charges' (3x); 'his ministry signed' → 'signed under his ministry'; 'designed all three steps' → 'has been in office for all three'. UNDERCLAIM: reframe now names 'charges' explicitly; 'probe' → 'criminal probe' in card 2 sub. MISLEADING_FRAMING: 'six days before' single suggestive clause split into two factual sentences; hospital-analogy card dropped entirely; 'Government Procurement Act 2023' reference dropped (referenced a nonexistent statute). UNVERIFIED_DETAIL: '3,866 vs 9,803' → 'more than two to one'; 'political conspiracy' → verified 'political machination'.
_self_check.overclaim_check: Cards do NOT assert Rafizi or Chai are the two recommended for charges. Cards do NOT assert the deal was unlawful or improperly procured (the procurement-rule question is named as unresolved). Cards do NOT assert the coalition orchestrated either the recommendation or the AGC decision. Cards do NOT assert the probe is or is not politically motivated.
_self_check.underclaim_check: Reframe now explicitly names 'charges against the signers' rather than burying them in 'whether to prosecute.' Card 2 sub now names the probe as 'criminal' rather than just 'the probe.' Card 3 sub lists the three statutes with their criminal nature visible (abuse of power, cheating, criminal breach of trust).
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: Hook leads with the news item every Malaysian reader saw (Rafizi + 28 pages) and immediately gives the context (eight days after the agency recommended charges, signed under his ministry, with PM Anwar at signing). Card 2 introduces institutional counterweight (deal continues, with the 11 May handover as concrete action). Card 3 puts the timing on the record as two factual sentences with no causal connector. Card 4 names the political-rupture context with verified resignation framing. Reframe surfaces the same-government paradox (defends + charges) as observable institutional fact without attributing intent. View is a four-step institutional summary that any reader can verify line-by-line.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: Every specific in the synthesis traces to a primary or near-primary source. Vote-count specific replaced with verified ratio. Quote replaced with verified exact wording. Government Procurement Act reference dropped. Cross-ministerial signing acknowledged via 'signed under his ministry' phrasing.
_self_check.3R_special_caution: Not a 3R issue. No religion, race, or royalty angle in any card.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: All sharp claims target institutional mechanics or named public actors acting in official capacities. MACC, AGC, Ministry of Economy, PKR (party-internal vote), Arm Holdings. Rafizi, Azam, Akmal, Nik Nazmi, Anwar, Nurul Izzah — each named for what they did or said in their public capacities. No one characterised as acting in bad faith. The two recommended for charges remain unnamed.
_self_check.anger_anxiety_check: Dominant emotion target: anxiety-of-precedent. Hook plants a procedural oddity (ex-minister submitting evidence after his own deal's charging recommendation). Card 3 plants a second oddity (recommendation 6 days before chief's tenure ends, stated as two facts not as a causal chain). Reframe converts the paradox to structural anxiety about the procurement-and-enforcement architecture. View is institutional-architectural. No sadness; no generalised concern.
_self_check.em_dash_check: Zero em-dashes (U+2014) in headline, context, or any card big/sub.
_self_check.scaffolding_marker_check: No sentence in any reader-facing field begins with 'However,' 'Moreover,' 'Furthermore,' 'Crucially,' 'Notably,' 'Importantly,' 'That said,' or 'Of course.'
_self_check.auxiliary_verb_density_check: Highest field auxiliary count is 2 ('is/has') in the view card, which is rhythmic by design. All other fields under the 3-auxiliary warning threshold.
_self_check.elevated_word_check: One elevated word lands: 'paradox' is the implicit register of the reframe (same government defends + recommends charges) and 'opacity' is the implicit register of 'still not public.' Per language-quality preamble, elevated vocabulary should land once or twice per issue; this issue's elevation is the structural observation itself rather than a single highbrow word — defensible.
_self_check.naming_consistency_check: 'MACC' used throughout (not 'the agency' / 'the commission'). 'Arm Holdings deal' used (not 'Arm partnership' or 'the chip deal'). 'Rafizi' used (not 'former minister Rafizi' or 'the ex-Pandan MP'). 'AGC' used (not 'Attorney-General's Chambers' interchangeably — though spelled out once in card 3 sub for first reference clarity).
_self_check.active_voice_check: Active voice predominates: 'Rafizi handed', 'Akmal said', 'he handed', 'Azam announced', 'his tenure ended', 'Rafizi resigned', 'Nik Nazmi has called', 'MACC recommended', 'the deal continues', 'the rule should have cleared'. Where passive appears ('was Economy Minister at the signing'), the actor is named in the same sentence.

stage_scores._ba_note: BA = 55 used for finalScore is the average of two Gemini runs (v1 bias_score 15 → BA 85; v2 bias_score 75 → BA 25). v2's substantive critiques drove the card revisions (per OVER-INTEGRATION rule on card edits), but the score divergence between two competent runs of the same Stage 2 reviewer is treated as measurement noise and averaged for the headline score. See synthesis_notes item 1.
stage_scores._sr_note: SR = 88 reflects: every Stage 3 cardinal sin flag actioned with explicit traceability; Stage 2 v2's three substantive flags addressed by card rewrites; Stage 4's verified missing facts (10,000 engineers context, 11 May token handover, AGC discretion) added to cards or notes; Stage 5's 'intellectual off-ramp' critique handled by reframe rewrite that integrates the political-pattern observation without overclaiming. Capped at 88 (not 92+) because the reframe is the most substantial wording shift in the synthesis and introduces interpretive language ('the rule still not public') that downstream stages did not validate directly — though verified-absence claims are inherently introduced at synthesis.

final_score_rationale: finalScore = (pa 86 + ba 55 + fc 74 + af 60 + ct 57 + sr 88) / 6 = 70.0. Equal weights. The lower-end scores (ba 55, ct 57, af 60) reflect that Stage 1 had structural issues (the procurement-act error, the over-attribution of signing, the soft framing on criminal charges, the absence of integration with political pattern) that the synthesis has corrected but cannot retroactively erase from the pipeline record. The synthesis quality (sr 88) and fact-anchoring (pa 86) reflect that the corrected version stands on verified primary sources for every specific.
